This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.

The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

description: This package contains configuration files for the default environments for Care-O-bot supported by IPA.

The cob_extern stack contains third party libraries needed for operating Care-O-bot. The packages are downloaded from the manufactorers website and not changed in any way.

This package wraps the external c++ library dlib (http://dlib.net/) in a ROS package, so other packages can use it. The code was obtained from https://github.com/davisking/dlib . For further descriptions and tutorials see the Makefile.tarball and http://dlib.net/ .

This package wraps the external c++ library opengm in a ROS package, so other packages can use it. It downloads the source code of it and then unzips it. The library is a header-only library with command line interfaces, which aren't used, so it doesn't gets build. For further descriptions and tutorials see the Makefile.tarball and https://github.com/opengm/opengm .

cob_map_accessibility_analysis receives the map from navigation as well as obstacles and inflates_obstacles topics to assemble a common obstacle map. description: CMake configuration to run coverage
readme: code_coverage ROS package run coverage testing Examples robot_calibration package generate coverage code Travis-CI Codecov.io Usage ROS package: Add code_coverage test depend package.xml Update CMakeLists.txt, testing section add NOTE order test targets coverage macros: if(CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING) find_package(code_coverage REQUIRED) Add compiler flags coverage instrumentation defining targets APPEND_COVERAGE_COMPILER_FLAGS() endif() Add targets (CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING) Add tests Create target ${PROJECT_NAME}_coverage_report if(ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING) set(COVERAGE_EXCLUDES "*/${PROJECT_NAME}/test*" "*/${PROJECT_NAME}/other_dir_i_dont_care_about*") add_code_coverage( ${PROJECT_NAME}_coverage_report DEPENDENCIES _run_tests_${PROJECT_NAME} endif() endif() Note: variable COVERAGE_EXCLUDES content! build run tests debug build reasonable coverage numbers): CATKIN_MAKE: catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report CATKIN_TOOLS: catkin config --cmake-args -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin build catkin build PACKAGE_NAME --no-deps --catkin-make-args PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report output print coverage report located Python rostest Support interface Python-based unit tests require modification coverage information, Python-based nodes run rostest launch files bit additional instrumentation turned <launch> Add argument launch file turn coverage <arg name="coverage" default="false"/> fancy line forces nodes generate coverage <arg name="pythontest_launch_prefix" value="$(eval 'python-coverage run arg('coverage') node generate coverage information <node pkg="example_pkg" name="publisher_node" type="publisher_node.py" node generate coverage <node pkg="example_pkg" name="subscriber_node" type="subscriber_node.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" test generate coverage information include launch-prefix <test time-limit="10" test-name="sample_rostest" pkg="example_pkg" type="sample_rostest.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" </launch> CMakeLists, pass argument: add_rostest(example_rostest.test ARGS coverage:=ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING)

description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Collada robot
    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.
authors: Rosen Diankov, Kei Okada, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

description:

readme: crane_x7_control CRANE-X7のためのパッケージ、 crane_x7_ros のcontrolパッケージです。 crane_x7_controlはU2D2(USB-RS485変換ケーブル)を使用してCRANE-X7のモータ制御を行います。 U2D2はLinuxに接続すると/dev/ttyUSB0として認識されるので、次のコマンドでアクセス権限を変更します。 sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 ケーブルの接続ポート名はデフォルトで/dev/ttyUSB0です。 /dev/ttyUSB1)を使う場合は次のコマンドを実行します。 roslaunch crane_x7_control crane_x7_control.launch port:=/dev/ttyUSB1 crane_x7_controlはデフォルト200Hz周期で制御しています。 制御周期を変更する場合はcrane_x7_control/src/hardware.cppを編集してください。 #define CONTROL_HZ rostopic /crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current subscribed [/crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current] average rate: min: max: std dev: window: average rate: min: max: std dev: window: 実際の制御周期が200Hzに達せず、100Hz程度に低くなってしまう場合、USB通信ポートのlatency_timerを変更してください。 cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer echo /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer 参考資料：https://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_wizard2/#usb-latency-setting crane_x7_controlは/crane_x7をルートとするネームスペースにパラメータやトピックを定義します。 各jointについて次のトピックを配信します。 current：電流値[mA] dxl_position：現在角度[360/4096度] temp：温度[度] dynamic_reconfigure crane_x7_controlはdynamic_reconfigureに対応しています。次のコマンドでrqt_reconfigureを起動してアクセスすると各Jointのサーボパラメータを変更することができます。 rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure 各パラメータの詳細についてはROBOTIS公式のXM430およびXM540のサーボマニュアルを参照して下さい。 XM430-W350 XM540-W270 CRANE-X7は位置制御モードと電流制御モードに対応しています。基本設定は位置制御モードになっていますが、複数箇所の設定変更を行うことで電流制御モードへ切り替えることが可能です。 サーボモータのOperating Mode（Address：11）を3：位置制御モードから0：電流制御モードへ設定変更する Dynamixel Wizard crane_x7_controlの設定変更 crane_x7_control/config/crane_x7_control.yamlのハンド部分について次のように変更する ```diff gripper_controller: type: “position_controllers/GripperActionController” type: “effort_controllers/GripperActionController” publish_rate: joint: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint gains: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: action_monitor_rate: state_publish_rate: stall_velocity_threshold: goal_tolerance: File truncated lines full file

description:
cv_camera uses OpenCV capture object to capture camera image.
  This supports camera_image and nodelet.
readme: ROS OpenCV camera driver easy capture video device cv::VideoCapture OpenCV. searching ROS2 driver, check cv_camera_node node camera_info_manager dealing camera_info. calibration data set, dummy values width height. Publish ~image_raw (sensor_msgs/Image) ~camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) Service ~set_camera_info (sensor_msgs/SetCameraInfo) Parameters ~rate (double, default: publish rate ~device_id (int, default: capture device ~device_path (string, default: path camera device file, /dev/video0. ~frame_id (string, default: “camera”) frame_id message header. ~image_width (int) set capture image width. ~image_height (int) set capture image height. ~camera_info_url (string) url camera info yaml. ~file (string, default: movie file device. ~capture_delay (double, default: estimated duration capturing receiving image. ~rescale_camera_info (bool, default: false) rescale camera calibration info automatically. ~camera_name (bool, default: frame_id) camera camera_info_manager. description:
The ddynamic_reconfigure_python package contains
    a class to instantiate dynamic reconfigure servers on the fly
    registering variables
readme: Dynamic dynamic reconfigure python Register variables dynamic reconfigure server run server fly. .cfg files CMakeLists.txt tinkering! import rospy ddynamic_reconfigure_python.ddynamic_reconfigure import DDynamicReconfigure def dyn_rec_callback(config, level): rospy.loginfo("Received reconf call: str(config)) return config '__main__': rospy.init_node('test_ddynrec') Create D(ynamic)DynamicReconfigure ddynrec DDynamicReconfigure("example_dyn_rec") Add variables description, default min, max, edit_method) ddynrec.add_variable("decimal", "float/double variable", ddynrec.add_variable("integer", "integer variable", ddynrec.add_variable("bool", "bool variable", True) ddynrec.add_variable("string", "string variable", "string dynamic variable") enum_method ddynrec.enum([ ddynrec.const("Small", "int", small constant"), ddynrec.const("Medium", "int", medium constant"), ddynrec.const("Large", "int", large constant"), ddynrec.const("ExtraLarge", "int", extra large constant")], enum ddynrec.add_variable("enumerate", "enumerate variable", edit_method=enum_method) Start server ddynrec.start(dyn_rec_callback) rospy.spin() rosrun ddynamic_reconfigure_python example.py. Run rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure play values. couple examples class scripts folder.

description:

This package is currently for internal use only. Its API may change
    without warning in the future.  This package is deprecated.

description:
Dynamixel-Workbench is dynamixel solution for ROS.
    This metapackage allows you to easily change the ID, baudrate and operating mode of the Dynamixel.
    Furthermore, it supports various controllers based on operating mode and Dynamixel SDK.
    These controllers are commanded by operators.
This package contains examples of applying the 'dynamixel_workbench_toolbox' library developed on the basis of dynamixel_sdk to various operating modes of Dynamixel.
This package contains nodes that control the Dynamixel by communicating with the server registered in the 'dynamixel_workbench_controllers' package

This package is composed of 'dynamixel_item', 'dynamixel_tool', 'dynamixel_driver' and 'dynamixel_workbench' class.
    The 'dynamixel_item' is saved as control table item and information of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_tool' class loads its by model number of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_driver' class includes wraped function used in DYNAMIXEL SDK.
    The 'dynamixel_workbench' class make simple to use Dynamixels
readme: run dynamixel_workbench linux ROS Downloads DynamixelSDK git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/DynamixelSDK Build DynamixelSDK http://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_sdk/library_setup/cpp_linux/#build-the-library Downloads Dynamixel-Workbench git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/dynamixel-workbench Build Dynamixel-Workbench Run examples ${YOUR_DOWNLOAD_PATH}/dynamixel_workbench/dynamixel_workbench_toolbox/examples mkdir build build cmake sudo chmod a+rw ${USB_PORT} ./monitor

description:
Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed. 

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).
     
     They will come as the need arises.

A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to 
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.
Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.
This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.
Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

Any tools relating to mathematical geometry. 
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing 
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these 
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it 
  to be done consistently across platforms.
Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots, 
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include 
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier 
     and are multithread-safe.
Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded 
     programming tools. These are usually different on different 
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support
	
	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.
Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.
Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.
Color codes for ansii consoles.

These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.
Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.
Tools and utilities for ecl development.

description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with
This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the

description:
The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.
hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.
hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt
hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

description: camera_calibration_parsers contains routines for reading and writing camera calibration parameters.

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.
Common code for working with images in ROS.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.
Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.
Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.
ImageZero is a fast lossless image compression algorithm for RGB color photos.

Compared PNG, compresses bit PPM files times faster, decompresses PPM times faster, compression rate good PNG natural photos. ImageZero suited “flat” (computer generated) graphics grayscale images. code “early work progress”, image format, library API fully outlined contact author, AUTHORS file. feedback appreciated. code originally https://github.com/cfeck/imagezero modified build shared library part catkin package.

ROS-Industrial core stack contains packages and libraries for supporing industrial systems

The Industrial deprecated package contains nodes, launch files, etc... that are slated for 
  deprecation.  This package is the last place something will end up before being deleted.  
  If you are missing a package/node and find it's contents here, then you should consider 
  a replacement.

The industrial message package containes industrial specific messages 
	definitions. This package is part of the ROS-Industrial program.

industrial robot client contains generic clients for connecting 
     to industrial robot controllers with servers that adhere to the
     simple message protocol.
The industrial robot simulator is a stand in for industrial robot driver node(s).  It adheres to the driver specification for industrial robot controllers.
Industrial utils is a library package that captures common funcitonality for the ROS-Industrial distribution.

simple_message defines a simple messaging connection and protocol for communicating 
	with an industrial robot controller.  Additional handler and manager classes are 
	included for handling connection limited systems.  This package is part of the ROS-Industrial 
	program.

ROS driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend
readme: ROS Driver Generic Linux Joysticks joy package joy_node, node interfaces generic Linux joystick ROS. node publishes “Joy” message, current state joystick’s buttons axes. Supported Hardware node work joystick supported Linux. Published Topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy): outputs joystick state. Device Selection parameters controlling device ~dev (string, default: “/dev/input/js0”) ~dev_name (string, default: (empty disabled)) ~dev_name empty, ~dev defines Linux joystick device read joystick events. ~dev_name defined, node enumerates joysticks, /dev/input/js*. joystick matching ~dev_name opened. joystick matches desired device device ~dev fallback. list names connected joysticks, invalid ~dev_name rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="*" output INFO]: joystick: LIS3LV02DL Accelerometer (/dev/input/js1). INFO]: joystick: Microsoft X-Box pad (/dev/input/js0). [ERROR]: find joystick Falling default device. node started device list. rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="Microsoft X-Box pad" Advanced Parameters ~deadzone (double, default: Amount joystick move considered off-center. parameter relative axis normalized means joystick move edge axis’s range axis output non-zero Linux deadzone processing, cases set ~autorepeat_rate (double, default: (disabled)) Rate joystick non-changing state resend previously message. ~coalesce_interval (double, default: Axis events received coalesce_interval (seconds) single ROS message. kernel sends axis motion separate event, coalescing greatly reduces rate messages option limit rate outgoing messages. Button events immediately avoid missing button presses. Information information Wiki page.

description:
This metapackage depends on packages for interfacing common
    joysticks and human input devices with ROS.

Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.
    Driver for the Sony PlayStation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3
    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens
    for a connection on the HID ports, starts the joystick
    streaming data, and passes the data to the Linux uinput device
    so that it shows up as a normal joystick.
authors: Blaise Gassend, pascal <AT> pabr.org, Melonee Wise

description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn

description: A simple viewer for ROS image topics with draw-on features
readme: image_view2 image_view2 extended image_view. Features Draw markers image view Interact user direction Interaction Modes image_view2 support mode interact user. set interaction mode ~interaction_mode parameter change mode service interfaces. Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Polygon mode (~interaction_mode:=poly) polygon mode, user select series closed lines. User add line left-click close lines right-click. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut) grabcut mode, user select curves. typing Esc key, clear selected curves. selected red curve published image/foreground (sensor_msgs/Image) green publiehd image/background (sensor_msgs/Image). mode originally implemented select forground background pixels grabcut. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. ROS API Publising Topics image/marked (sensor_msgs/Image) Image marks drawed. image/screenrectangle_image (sensor_msgs/Image) Cropped image user selection Rectangle mode. Subscribing Topics image (sensor_msgs/Image) Input image camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) CameraInfo image. camera info required compute projection matrix drawing markers. image_marker (image_view2/ImageMarker2) Marker topic. image/event (image_view2/MouseEvent) Event interface. image_view2 handle mouse events gui interaction. topic emulate event handling ros topics File truncated lines full file

The rosbag tools
readme: jsk_rosbag_tools Tools creating video rosbag compressing rosbag images. bag_to_video.py Create video rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_video.py [--fps FPS] [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] [--image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC input_bagfile rosbag video positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output directory path filename. --image-topic interpreted directory file --fps FPS --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC --image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC Topic extract. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_video.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio --image-topic /head_camera/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color/compressed /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.mp4 bag_to_audio.py Create audio file rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_audio.py [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] input_bagfile rosbag audio positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output filename. `--audio-topic`_info exists, samplerate channels. --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_audio.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.wav video_to_bag.py Convert video file bagfile. Usage usage: video_to_bag.py output_file] [--topic-name TOPIC_NAME] [--compress] [--no-progress-bar] inputvideo Convert video bag. positional arguments: inputvideo optional arguments: show message exit output_file, output_file output bag file --topic-name TOPIC_NAME Converted topic --compress Compress Image flag. --no-progress-bar show progress bar. The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

description: Convert urdf mesh.
readme: URDF Mesh Converter package converts colored output URDF Exporter plugin SolidWorks format readable ROS systems. Specifically, package generates colored .dae models .3dxml files. JSK users refer URDF Exporter documentation guidance. Quick Start building package, source workspace. simply execute command .3dxml URDF file path desired output URDF path. rosrun urdf_mesh_converter convert_urdf_mesh.py <YOUR_URDF_PATH> --output <OUTPUT_URDF_PATH> practical apply package actual URDF running command: rosrun urdf_mesh_converter convert_urdf_mesh.py $(rospack find urdf_mesh_converter)/samples/pitch_yaw_2axis_camera_module.urdf --output $(rospack find urdf_mesh_converter)/samples/pitch_yaw_2axis_camera_module_modified.urdf

description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. readme: Sound Classification ROS package classify sound stream. Contents Setup Usage Quick demo Setup Install ROS. Ubuntu Ubuntu Create workspace mkdir ~/sound_classification_ws/src ~/sound_classification_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition.git rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src catkin build sound_classification source ~/sound_classification_ws/devel/setup.bash Install packages. cuda cupy needed chainer. installation guide JSK GPU highly recommended. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Save environmental noise train_data/noise.npy. subtracting noise, spectrograms clear. script, give sound sensor. update noise data everytime sound recognition, environmental sound differs everytime. noise samples roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Publish audio spectrum spectrogram topics. set max/min frequency included spectrum high_cut_freq/low_cut_freq args audio_to_spectrogram.launch. gui:=true, spectrum spectrogram visualized. roslaunch sound_classification audio_to_spectrogram.launch gui:=true spectrogram quiet environment. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. Rosbag version (Recommended) recommend rosbag collect spectrograms. rosbag easy save_sound.launch parameters. target_class:=TARGET_CLASS, set class target sound. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG, save spectrograms rosbag. default, rosbag paused Press ‘Space’ key terminal start playing rosbag. rosbag ends, press ‘Ctrl-c’ terminate. newly saved spectrograms appended existing spectrograms. change threshold sound saving threshold:=xxx. smaller easily sound saved. Save audio rosbag roslaunch sound_classification record_audio_rosbag.launch filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG play rosbag collecting data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 setting `threshold:=0` `save_when_sound:=false`, collect spectrogram sound". ```bash File truncated lines full file

readme: kvh-geo-fog-3d-driver Driver KVH GEO FOG inertial navigation systems. Connects serial KVH GEO FOG device publishes KVH-specific generic ROS-ified messages, performs basic transforms convert data ROS-conformant conventions. Initial testing MITRE Corporation “Dual” model, dual-antenna heading solutions. API match dual non-dual models. detailed information KVH GEO FOG functionality, consult technical reference manual. Product pages: KVH GEO FOG KVH GEO FOG Dual Copyright MITRE Corporation. Rights Reserved. ROS API kvh_geo_fog_3d_driver_node main driver node. Published Topics KVH-specific messages messages defined part kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs package. direct pass-throughs KVH messages, universally conform ROS REPs KVH reports latitude longitude radians. ~<node_name>/kvh_system_state (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DSystemState) ~<node_name>/kvh_satellites (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DSatellites) ~<node_name>/kvh_detailed_satellites (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DDetailSatellites) ~<node_name>/kvh_local_magnetics (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DLocalMagneticField) ~<node_name>/kvh_utm_position (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DUTMPosition) ~<node_name>/kvh_ecef_pos (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DECEFPos) ~<node_name>/kvh_north_seeking_status (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DNorthSeekingInitStatus) ~<node_name>/kvh_odometer_state (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DSatellites) ~<node_name>/kvh_raw_sensors (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DRawSensors) ~<node_name>/kvh_raw_gnss (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DRawGNSS) Conventional ROS messages messages conform ROS REP-105 REP-145. “ROS Conformance Conventions” section details. Generally speaking, “_flu” “_enu” suffix conforms ROS REPs. angles measured radians suffixed “_deg”. NavSatFix latitude/longitude measured degrees message definition). KVH messages, report latitude/longitude radians. ~<node_name>/imu/data_raw_frd (sensor_msgs/Imu) ~<node_name>/imu/data_raw_flu (sensor_msgs/Imu) REP-105, REP-145 compliant ~<node_name>/imu/data_ned (sensor_msgs/Imu) ~<node_name>/imu/data_enu (sensor_msgs/Imu) REP-105, REP-145 compliant ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_ned (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_ned_deg (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_enu (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_enu_deg (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/gps/fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) Filtered GNSS location, degrees ~<node_name>/gps/raw_fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) Raw GNSS location, degrees ~<node_name>/gps/mag (sensor_msgs/MagneticField) ~<node_name>/gps/utm_ned (nav_msgs/Odometry) ~<node_name>/gps/utm_enu (nav_msgs/Odometry) REP-105 compliant ~<node_name>/odom/wheel_encoder (nav_msgs/Odometry) ~<node_name>imu/raw_sensor_frd (nav_msgs/Odometry) ~<node_name>/odom/raw_sensor_flu (nav_msgs/Odometry) REP-105 compliant Parameters driver asks wheel odometer pulses meter passed parameter. KVH API odometer pulses directionally pulse count decreases) odometer distance absolute distance traveled backing increases distance traveled, negative). source non-differentiated data, fuse position filters doesn’t drift time velocity, drift). KVH API provide method obtaining current wheel odometer pulse distance hand-coded parameter. future, KVH exposes API. ~<node_name>/port (string, default: /dev/ttyUSB0) ~<node_name>/baud (int, default: ~<node_name>/debug (bool, default: false) ~<node_name>/filterVehicleType (string, default: (car, spatial_packets.h)) ~<node_name>/atmosphericAltitudeEnabled (bool, default: true) ~<node_name>/velocityHeadingEnabled (bool, default: false) ~<node_name>/reversingDetectionEnabled (bool, default: true) ~<node_name>/motionAnalysisEnabled (bool, default: true) File truncated lines full file

ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.

description:
The log_view package provides a ncurses based terminal GUI for
    viewing and filtering published ROS log messages.

    This is an alternative to rqt_console and swri_console that doesn't depend
    on qt and can be run directly in a terminal.
readme: log_view log_viewer ncurses text viewing rosout logs. Motivation Receive, filter, navigate published rosout log messages terminal support debugging robot live. Problem rosout log messages don’t printed terminal normal lots nodes/messages. convienient filter navigate real time. swri_console desired functionality based requires graphical interface. Solution log_viewer similar concept design swri_console, ncurses interface. Log messages filtered based log level node whitelist text filter exclude text filter log_viewer supports text searches jumping match match. Log messages copied clipboard selecting swri_console, log_viewer doesn’t roscore start automatically connect/reconnect roscore Keybindings CTRL-c Exit log viewer CTRL-h Show/hide screen Arrow Scroll Arrow Scroll Left Arrow Scroll left Arrow Scroll Page Scroll screen Page Scroll screen Home Scroll message Scroll message follow Tab Change focus input Show/hide debug level Show/hide info level Show/hide warning level Show/hide error level Show hide fatal level Enable/disable node filter CTRL-a Select log lines copy clipboard CTRL-n Show/hide node selection CTRL-s Search matching string CTRL-x Clear search Backspace Prev match Enter match CTRL-e Enable/disable text exclude filter CTRL-f Enable/disable text include filter Mouse Support: limited mouse support selecting log messages enabling/disabling log level node filters. Due bug distributed version ncurses, mousewheel scrolling works direction. Improvements: Regular expression support ROS2 support Support loading logs bag files Support loading logs ROS log directory Refine design keybindings consistent/intuitive

Supported schemas: Serial: /path/to/serial/device[:baudrate] Serial: serial:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] Serial hardware flow control: serial-hwfc:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] UDP: udp://[bind_host][:port]@[remote_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] UDP broadcast GCS discovery: udp-b://[bind_host][:port]@[:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] UDP broadcast (permanent): udp-pb://[bind_host][:port]@[:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP client: tcp://[server_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP server: tcp-l://[bind_port][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] Note: ids URL overrides ids system_id component_id parameters. Dependencies mavros: Linux host Boost Boost.ASIO) console-bridge library compiller support License Licensed terms LGPLv3, BSD, GPLv3.

description: 3-D/6-DOF localization for mobile robots with 3-D LIDAR(s)
readme: mcl_3dl [!NOTE] official ROS EOL, package released Alpine ROS. Package summary mcl_3dl ROS node perform probabilistic 3-D/6-DOF localization system mobile robots LIDAR(s). implements pointcloud based Monte Carlo localization reference pointcloud map. node receives reference pointcloud environment map localizes 6-DOF yaw, pitch, roll) pose measured pointclouds assisted motion prediction odometry. supported motion model differential-wheeled-robot. node classic MCL; doesn’t implement adaptive feature KDL-sampling Algorithms fundamental algorithm mcl_3dl node Monte Carlo localization (MCL), aka particle filter localization. MCL represents probabilistic distribution estimated pose density weight particles estimates pose distribution. Algorithm details Parameters Node Install source Note: mcl_3dl_msgs package required build mcl_3dl package. clone /path/to/your/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl.git git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl_msgs.git build /path/to/your/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Install dependencies catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Release build recommended apt repository ROS Indigo/Kinetic/Lunar Ubuntu) sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-mcl-3dl Running demo bag file 2+4-DOF tracked vehicle Hokuyo YVT-X002 LIDAR online. Pre-processed (filtered) pointcloud, IMU pose, odometry, map data packed bag. Download bag wget ~/Downloads https://openspur.org/~atsushi.w/dataset/mcl_3dl/short_test3.bag Running demo roslaunch mcl_3dl test.launch use_pointcloud_map:=false use_cad_map:=false use_bag_file:=true bag_file:=${HOME}/Downloads/short_test3.bag map data bag generated cartographer_ros filtered pcl_outlier_removal pcl_voxel_grid utilities. MarkerArray shows mcl_3dl internal information. Purple spheres: sampled points likelihood-model calculation Red lines: casted rays beam-model calculation Red boxes: detected collisions raycasting global localization, call /global_localization command. rosservice call /global_localization Demos odometry IMU Contributing mcl_3dl package developed GitHub flow. Feel free open Issue and/or Pull Request. code repository ROS Style Guide. configuration file clang-format https://github.com/seqsense/ros_style/. License mcl_3dl BSD license. Backport codes Point Cloud Library (PCL) BSD license.

description: The mongodb_store_msgs package

This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the AbstractPlanner, AbstractController and AbstractRecovery plugin interfaces. This interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make the plugin available for Move Base Flex. The abstract classes provides a meaningful interface enabling the planners, controllers and recovery behaviors to return information, e.g. why something went wrong. Derivided interfaces can, for example, provide methods to initialize the planner, controller or recovery with map representations like costmap_2d, grid_map or other representations.
readme: Move Base Flex Abstract Core mbf_abstract_core package Move Base Flex (MBF) abstract interfaces planning controlling recovering. Derived packages mbf_abstract_nav derived interfaces mbf_abstract_core method initialization plugin, handing pointer map representation choice. plugins loaded Move Base Flex derived package mbf_abstract_nav) implement derived interfaces interfaces defined package. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractPlanner interface planner plugin plan global paths. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractController interface controller plugin control move robot. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractRecovery interface recovery plugin recover robot deadlocked situations.

description: The mbf_abstract_nav package contains the abstract navigation server implementation of Move Base Flex (MBF). The abstract navigation server is not bound to any map representation. It provides the actions for planning, controlling and recovering. MBF loads all defined plugins at the program start. Therefor, it loads all plugins which are defined in the lists *planners*, *controllers* and *recovery_behaviors*. Each list holds a pair of a *name* and a *type*. The *type* defines which kind of plugin to load. The *name* defines under which name the plugin should be callable by the actions.
readme: Move Base Flex Abstract Navigation Server mbf_abstract_nav package abstract navigation server implementation Move Base Flex (MBF). abstract navigation server bound map representation. Actions planning, controlling recovering. time start MBF loads defined plugins. Therefor, loads plugins defined lists planners, controllers recovery_behaviors. list holds pair type. type defines kind plugin load. defines plugin callable actions.

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the CostmapPlanner, CostmapController and CostmapRecovery interfaces. The interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make them available for Move Base Flex using the mbf_costmap_nav navigation implementation. That implementation inherits the mbf_abstract_nav implementation and binds the system to a local and a global costmap.

description: The mbf_utility package

Move Base Flex (MBF) is a backwards-compatible replacement for move_base. MBF can use existing plugins for move_base, and provides an enhanced version of the planner, controller and recovery plugin ROS interfaces. It exposes action servers for planning, controlling and recovering, providing detailed information of the current state and the plugin’s feedback. An external executive logic can use MBF and its actions to perform smart and flexible navigation strategies. Furthermore, MBF enables the use of other map representations, e.g. meshes or grid_map
       
       This package is a meta package and refers to the Move Base Flex stack packages.The abstract core of MBF – without any binding to a map representation – is represented by the
The move_base_sequence package

description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor

description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.
Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.
Contains a set of tutorials that run 2D-Navigation within Stage-Simulator.

The assisted_teleop node subscribes to a desired trajectory topic
    (geometry_msgs/Twist) and uses TrajectoryPlannerROS to find a valid
    trajectory close to the desired trajectory before republishing. Useful for
    filtering teleop commands while avoiding obstacles. This package also
    contains LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, which is a LaserScan filter plugin that
    takes max range values in a scan and turns them into valid values that are
    slightly less than max range.
readme: assisted_teleop assisted_teleop node subscribes desired trajectory topic (geometry_msgs/Twist) TrajectoryPlannerROS find valid trajectory close desired trajectory republishing. filtering teleop commands avoiding obstacles. package LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, LaserScan filter plugin takes max range values scan turns valid values slightly max range.

description: Object_recognition_msgs contains the ROS message and the actionlib definition used in object_recognition_core

The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

The OctoMap library implements a 3D occupancy grid mapping approach, providing data structures and mapping algorithms in C++. The map implementation is based on an octree. See
  http://octomap.github.io for details.

Pinocchio analytical derivatives main Rigid-Body Algorithms, Recursive Newton-Euler Algorithm Articulated-Body Algorithm. Pinocchio tailored robotics applications, contexts (biomechanics, computer graphics, vision, built Eigen linear algebra FCL collision detection. Pinocchio Python interface fast code prototyping, accessible Conda. Pinocchio heart robotics software Crocoddyl, open-source efficient Differential Dynamic Programming solver robotics, Stack-of-Tasks, open-source versatile hierarchical controller framework Humanoid Path Planner, open-source software Motion Manipulation Planning. learn Pinocchio internal behaviors main features, invite read related paper online documentation. dive Pinocchio single line sufficient (assuming Conda): conda install pinocchio conda-forge pip Linux): pip install pin Table contents Table contents Pinocchio main features Documentation Examples Tutorials Pinocchio continuous integrations Performances Ongoing developments Installation ROS Visualization Citing Pinocchio Questions Issues Core-dev team Credits Open-source projects relying Pinocchio Acknowledgments Pinocchio main features Pinocchio fast: template library, cache friendly, support custom scalar type. Pinocchio versatile, implementing basic advanced rigid body dynamics algorithms: kinematics analytical derivatives, forward/inverse dynamics analytical derivatives, centroidal dynamics analytical derivatives, computations kinematic dynamic regressors system identification full support closed-loop mechanisms, state-of-the-art frictional contact solvers, low-complexity constrained articulated body algorithms, sparse constrained dynamics analytical derivatives, full support multiple-precision floating-point (MPFR) Python support modern open-source Automatic Differentiation frameworks CppAD CasADi, automatic code generation support CppADCodeGen. Pinocchio create Multi-body system URDF file, SDF file, MJCF file, SRDF file add frame contact. Pinocchio flexible: header compliant. Pinocchio extensible. Pinocchio multi-thread friendly. The pr2_gripper_action provides an action interface for using the
  gripper. Users can specify what position to move to (while limiting the
  force) and the action will report success when the position is reached or
  failure when the gripper cannot move any longer.

The PR2 head action is a node that provides an action interface for
  pointing the head of the PR2.  It passes trajectory goals to the
  controller, and reports success when they have finished executing.

description:
The single joint position action is a node that provides an action

The pr2_gripper_sensor package

The pr2_gripper_sensor_action package provides an action interface for talking to the pr2_gripper_sensor_controller real-time controller.

  It provides several different actions for getting high-level sensor information from the PR2 palm-mounted accelerometers, fingertip pressure arrays, and gripper motor/encoder, as well as several sensor-based gripper control actions that respond with low-latency in real-time.

description: The pr2_gripper_sensor_controller package is a real-time controller that integrates signals from the PR2 hand-mounted accelerometer and finger-mounted pressure sensors with motor control of the gripper joint to do highly responsive sensing and low-latency closed-loop control.

description:
The pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs package contains various actions and messages that are used in the pr2_gripper_sensor* packages. The structure of the API used by pr2_gripper_sensor_action, and pr2_gripper_sensor_controller packages is as follows: 

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

The controller manager (CM) package provides the infrastructure to run controllers in a hard realtime loop.
authors: Eric Berger berger <AT> willowgarage.com, Stuart Glaser, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains the C++ interfaces to the PR2 hardware
  components that are controlled over EtherCAT. This includes the
  motors and encoders needed to control the PR2 mechanism, as well as
  components like the pressure sensors in the fingertips, camera
  triggers, etc... All of the hardware components in this interface are
  directly available to the controllers inside the hard realtime
  control loop.
The pr2_mechanism stack contains the infrastructure to control the PR2 robot in a hard realtime control loop.
The `pr2_mechanism_diagnostics` node subscribes to `mechanism_statistics` and publishes diagnostics data for joints and controllers on `/diagnostics`.

authors: Eric Berger berger <AT> willowgarage.com, Stuart Glaser, Wim Meeussen

This package provides a node that monitors the state of the run stops of the pr2_robot. When the state of the

description: quaternion operations
readme: pyquaternion fully featured, pythonic library quaternion representation, manipulation, animation geometry. visit pyquaternion homepage full information latest documentation. Designed Python Smooth animation interpolation random orientations pyquaternion module. Quickstart Install PyPI pip install pyquaternion Run basic overview. copy [demo.py](./demo/demo.py). ```python import pyquaternion Create quaternion representing rotation degrees positive axis. my_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, degrees=90) my_vector my_rotated_vector my_quaternion.rotate(my_vector) print('\nBasic Rotation') print('--------------') print('My Vector: {}'.format(my_vector)) print('Performing rotation {angle} deg {axis}'.format(angle=my_quaternion.degrees, axis=my_quaternion.axis)) print('My Rotated Vector: {}'.format(my_rotated_vector)) Create quaternion representing rotation null_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, angle=0) print('\nInterpolated Rotation') print('---------------------') create sequence intermediate quaternion rotation objects pyquaternion.Quaternion.intermediates(null_quaternion, my_quaternion, include_endpoints=True): my_interpolated_point q.rotate(my_vector) print('My Interpolated Point: {point}\t(after rotation {angle} deg {axis})'.format( point=my_interpolated_point, angle=round(q.degrees, axis=q.axis print('Done!') output: Basic Rotation Vector: Performing rotation deg Rotated Vector: Interpolated Rotation Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg

description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.
authors: Dave Hershberger, Dorian Scholz, Dirk Thomas

description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.

description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

description:
Contains a set of tools that can be used from a hard
    realtime thread, without breaking the realtime behavior.  The
    tools currently only provides the realtime publisher, which makes
    it possible to publish messages to a ROS topic from a realtime
    thread. We plan to add a basic implementation of a realtime
    buffer, to make it possible to get data from a (non-realtime)
    topic callback into the realtime loop. Once the lockfree buffer is
    created, the realtime publisher will start using it, which will
    result in major API changes for the realtime publisher (removal of
    all lock methods).

description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

     convert raw depth/RGB/IR streams to depth images, disparity images, 
     and (registered) point clouds.
