The lockfree package contains lock-free data structures for use in multithreaded programming.  These
     kinds of data structures are generally not as easy to use as single-threaded equivalents, and are not
     always faster.  If you don't know you need to use one, try another structure with a lock around it
     first.
The ros_realtime package

rosatomic provides the C++11-style atomic operations by pulling symbols from the proposed Boost.Atomic
     package into the ros namespace.  Once C++11-style atomics (std::atomic) are available from compilers, rosatomic will
     conditionally use those instead.

Usage depend rosfmt catkin package usual. catch fmt requires enable cmake_minimum_required(VERSION project(my_package) find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS rosfmt roscpp rosconsole required older versions rosfmt catkin_package() include_directories(${catkin_INCLUDE_DIRS}) Important: enable set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD add_executable(my_node src/my_node.cpp target_link_libraries(my_node ${catkin_LIBRARIES} License rosfmt underlying fmt library licensed BSD-2 license.

The rt_usb_9axisimu_driver package
readme: English rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverは株式会社アールティが販売しているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュール用のROSパッケージです。 License: 3-Clause BSD License Source: https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git (branch: master) 現在、以下のROSのディストリビューションに対応しております。 Kinetic (kinetic-devel) Melodic (melodic-devel) Noetic (noetic-devel) Dashing (dashing-devel) Foxy (foxy-devel) industrial_ci source build amd64 binary arm64 binary main develop(master) ROS develop(ros2-devel) Xenial Kinetic(kinetic-devel) Bionic Melodic(melodic-devel) Focal Noetic(noetic-devel) Bionic Dashing(dashing-devel) Focal Foxy(foxy-devel) このパッケージは、USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールをROSから使用するためのドライバを提供するものです。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールは、センサとしてInvenSense社のMPU9250を使用しております。 このセンサの磁気センサの座標系はNED座標系(x-north, y-east, z-down)ですが、 モジュール内のマイコン(LPC1343)においてENU座標系(x-east, y-north, z-up)に変換され、 これはROSで使われる座標系のルールにも適合しています。詳しくは、REP-0103をご覧ください。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはオープンハード・オープンソースのため、モジュール内のマイコンのファームウェアの変更が可能です。 このROSパッケージはデフォルトのファームウェアにのみ対応しております。ファームウェアを変更された場合、正常な動作ができなくなる恐れがございますので、ご了承ください。 ver2.0でのご利用について 2018年10月現在、販売されているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはver2.0となります。 このバージョンのデフォルトのファームウェアには、ASCII出力とBinary出力の２つのデータ出力形式があります。 センサ出荷時点ではASCII出力に設定されています。出力形式の切り替え方法は、以下のリポジトリにあるマニュアルをご参照ください。 https://github.com/rt-net/RT-USB-9AXIS-00 ROS 2のパッケージについてはros2-develのREADMEをご覧ください。 ROS Kineticの場合 sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver ROS Melodicの場合 sudo apt install ros-melodic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver catkinワークスペースを~/catkin_wsとすると、以下のような手順になります。 ~/catkin_ws/src git clone $ROS_DISTRO-devel https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin build 2のようにインストールを行った場合、ドライバを起動しようとする端末(Terminal)で、 source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roscore rosrun rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver roslaunch rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.launch [ERROR] Error opening sensor device, re-check devices. File truncated lines full file

This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
    to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
    The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
    the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
    separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

This repository includes ros packages to plan routes for multiple robots on a search graph. It creates a search graph out of a pixel map and tries to find a path for multiple robots using an extended approach for prioritized planning. The inputs are the tuw_multi_robot_msgs/RobotInfo messages which include the robots pose, the map and the desired goal poses. The output are multiple synchronized routes given to the individual robots.
maintainers: Benjamin Binder, Markus Bader, Florian Beck

description:
This contains CvBridge, which converts between ROS
    Image messages and OpenCV images.

`image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.
Tests the enumerants of the ROS Image message, and functionally tests the Python and C++ implementations of CvBridge.
Packages for interfacing ROS with OpenCV, a library of programming functions for real time computer vision.
Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode of Flash code pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode Flash code pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode Flash code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Project webpage ros.org: tutorial API reference Project webpage: source code download, bug report Setup package submodules. compiled ROS package catkin_make. Prerequisities visp_auto_tracker depends visp_bridge visp_tracker packages https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp (indigo-devel branches). visp_auto_tracker depends libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev system dependencies. install run: sudo apt-get install libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev build visp_tracker Supposed catkin work space run: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone indigo-devel https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp.git catkin_make --pkg visp_auto_tracker Documentation documentation project ROS homepage. information, refer ROS tutorial.

description:

      1. Recording of scenes
      2. Training of an ISM tree (Implicit shape model tree)
      3. Recognition of scenes
      4. Visualization of ISM (tree) data 
	  etc.

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in

description:
This package contains a system to recognize scenes called the Probabilistic Scene Model (PSM).
    It uses objects and relative poses (relations) between the objects. The realations can be dynamic and each object can be a reference object.
    The system consists of a training subsystem that trains new scenes and a scene inference subsystem that calculates scene probabilities of previously trained scenes.
authors: Braun Kai, Gehrung Joachim, Heizmann Heinrich, Meißner Pascal
readme: General Acquisition: Call "start_cameras.launch" "start_recognition_system.launch" start add required nodes visualization, cameras, coordinate transformations object detectors. record data processing online learning scene models), call script "start_recording_localizer_input_and_results.sh SEQUENCE_ID" "start_recording_objects_only.sh SEQUENCE_ID". resulting file called "OBJ_SEQUENCE_ID.bag". Scene Model Learning: scene: Call "roslaunch asr_psm start_scene_graph_generation.launch scene_id:=SCENE_NAME" launch directory. scene graph generator started launch file collects published object messages processes means online data rosbag file Press key 'p' publish results. account, object type scene supported. holds objects type multiple trajectory recordings scene. script "start_recording_scene_graph.sh SEQUENCE_ID" scripts directory capture output. resulting file called "SCENEGRAPH_SEQUENCE_ID.bag". learn model bagfile(s) launch file "learner.launch" launch "roslaunch asr_psm learner.launch". Inference: Start image aquisition "roslaunch asr_psm start_cameras.launch". Start object detectors "roslaunch asr_psm start_recognition_system.launch". Edit "inference.launch" file fit launch command "roslaunch asr_psm inference.launch". results visualized RVIZ, demand gnuplot graph showing scene probabilities summoned.

description:
This package includes the algorithmic link between object pose prediction and scene recognition, both based on ISM trees. While it only refers to other packages with respect to the aforementioned
    functionalities, it includes the algorithms realizing Scene Model Sampling. E.g., it provides a (service-based) interface for the scene recognition, including an ASR-optimized visualization of ISM trees.
    The reason for linking scene recognition and object pose prediction within a package instead of message passing, is the size and number of scene recognition results occurring.

readme: asr_recognizer_prediction_ism documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_recognizer_prediction_ism

description: This package provides object pose prediction on the basis of the alternative scene model PSM (Probabilistic Scene Model): It generates hypotheses (type and pose) for missing objects in the context of 3D object search. Moreover, it integrates scene recognition and object pose prediction (both on the basis of PSM) into Active Scene Recognition. Its output are asr_next_best_view/AttributedPointCloud.msg messages which can be processed by asr_next_best_view.

description:
This package implements the logic which connects different functionalities such as (passive) scene recognition, object pose prediction and Next-Best-View estimation
    to Active Scene Recognition. In particular, it is the interface for executing different 3D-object-search procedures one of which Active Scene Recognition is.
    Active Scene Recognition is equivalent to the "object search", which consists of the "direct search" and "indirect search" modes. The search will be executed by
    using different packages of the asr framework.

description:
This package implements the logic of our Online World Model, while the Offline Knowledge Base is distributed across asr_object_database, mild_navigation and asr_visualization_server. It contains four types of information:
      1. All estimations of objects which were localized by object detection (object models)
      2. All views in which objects of given types have been searched due to advice of asr_next_best_view and asr_direct_search_manager
      3. All recognized complete patterns by the recognizer_prediction_ism (scene models)
      4. Some additional information about the Objects: IntermediateObjectWeight, RecognizerName

Visit AWS RoboMaker website learn building intelligent robotic applications Amazon Web Services. Install source code workspace. Add .rosinstall file contents workspace folder run rosws update: git: {local-name: src/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs, uri: 'https://github.com/aws-robotics/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs.git', version: master} Add dependency package.xml access service types (.srv): <package> <depend>aws_robomaker_simulation_ros_pkgs</depend> </package> Follow Python usage build bundle workspace usual: build ROS rosws update rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src colcon build colcon bundle Usage tag, untag, list tags simulation job ROS command-line ROS application running. AWS RoboMaker documentation. IAM role permissions Replace account# account number. "Version": "Statement": "Action": "robomaker:TagResource", "robomaker:UntagResource", "robomaker:ListTagsForResource" "Resource": "arn:aws:robomaker:*:account#:simulation-job/*" "Effect": AddTags Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.msg import Tag robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import AddTags def add_tags_to_my_sim(): add_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def add_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/add_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestAddTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/add_tags', AddTags) response requestAddTags(tags) response.success: AddTags failed print response.message RemoveTag Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import RemoveTags def remove_tags_from_my_sim(): remove_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def remove_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestRemoveTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', RemoveTags) response requestRemoveTags(tags) response.success: RemoveTags failed print response.message File truncated lines full file

This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.

GNU General Public License details. received copy GNU General Public License program; write Free Software Foundation, Temple Place Suite Boston, USA. library encoporates ideas software libraries: Scene (Andrew Davison). <http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~ajd/Scene/> Bayes++ ACFR). <http://www.acfr.usyd.edu.au/technology/bayesianfilter/Bayes++.htm> CES programming library (Sebastian Thrun). <http://www-2.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs.cmu.edu/user/thrun/public_html/papers/thrun.ces-tr.html> research Bayesian methods compliant motion problems <http://www.mech.kuleuven.be/pma/research/manip/default_en.phtml> important features Released GNU LGPL licence Wrapper matrix RNG libraries, favourite matrix library. wrappers exist matrix/RNG wrapper library LTIlib <http://ltilib.sourceforge.net/doc/homepage/index.shtml>: library algorithms data structures frequently image processing computer vision. NEWMAT <http://www.robertnz.net/nm_intro.htm> Matrix Library boost <http://www.boost.org/> RNG "Bayesian unifying Design". incorporate Bayesian filtering algorithm! filter schemes implemented. Standard EKF, IEKF Non-minimal State <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~tlefebvr/publicaties/BayesStat.ps.gz> Standard Particle filter (arbitrary proposal), BootstrapFilter (Proposal System Model PDF), Auxiliary Particle filter, Extended Kalman Particle Filter. details design ideas, poster library presented Valencia conference Bayesian Statistics, <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~kgadeyne/doctoraat.html> filtering libraries home page <http://www.orocos.org/bfl> Tinne Laet Contributed tutorial website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/getting_started_guide/getting_started_guide.html> discusses construct filter bfl. Wim Meeussen Tinne Laet contributed installation guide website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/installation_guide/installation_guide.html>

Published boxes, scores, classes. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). segmentation type cnn_bridge/Netmask Segmentation data. Published 2-dimensional array mask values. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). True saves ‘/Camera__datetime'. string video title. node saves raw video video mask boxes overlay. running video file, raw saved. cpu type string default False implemented! Sets run network CPU Nvidia GPU present. gpu_percent type Float default Sets percentage Nvidia GPU generally running simultaneous networks. This package provides nodes that broadcast tf-frames along various (model-based) Cartesian paths (e.g. Linear, Circular).

The cob_frame_tracker package contains nodes that publish Twist commands based on the distance to the desired tf frame target.
The cob_hardware_emulation package provides idealized nodes emulating real robot hardware and/or physics simulation.

    The out/input for the wheel command/state are wheel velocities in rad/s for the wheels 
    [front left, front right, rear left, rear right]

description: The cob_obstacle_distance package calculates distances between both robot links and obstacles to be used for obstacle avoidance within cob_twist_controller framework.
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot omni-directional base platform.
This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot tricycle base platform.

The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

description: This package contains classes that are able to control the platform of the Care-O-Bot. This means to establish a CAN communication to drive and steering motors of the platform and later send motion commands and receive motor information.

The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".
The cob_driver stack includes packages that provide access to the Care-O-bot hardware through ROS messages, services and actions. E.g. for mobile base, arm, camera sensors, laser scanners, etc...
This packagae implements the special homing procedure that is needed for old cob4/raw bases

The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.

description: This package implements a sound play module using text2wave and aplay through python.

cob_undercarriage_ctrl implements a controller for the omnidirectional base of Care-O-bot 3 on joint level. For a given Pltf-Twist the according wheel steering angles and linear wheel velocities are calculated based on the principle of rigid body motion. Each joint is than controlled individually to achieve the computed position and velocity

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

A controller ensuring the safe operation of Kobuki.

Supported formats: .obj .ply CAD files .pcd scanned files. node main node perform alignment FOD detection. opens subscribers clouds topics start process. procedure based Finite State Machine states shown package wiki. Pre Alignment process process detecting FODs based comparing pointclouds. step clouds registered PCL Registration API. achieved pre alignment process iterative algorithm called GICP. pre alignment step reduces GICP process time vary effectiveness depending input clouds. package, methods alignment presented. prefered method set launch starting inspection procedure. HARRIS. method focused finding corners set keypoints. BOUNDARY. method detects parts edges set keypoints. MULTISCALE. method able find keypoints relevance scales. recommended compairing cloud previous scan. NORMALS. in-house method based relation point clouds normals. finds correspondences orientation dominant normals. avoid pre alignment process clouds close start inmediatly GICP. Usage load_clouds publish clouds. Clouds path param /pointcloud_folder. examples pointclouds. rosrun leica_point_cloud_processing load_clouds rosservice call /publish_clouds "source_cloud_file: 'scan_fods.pcd' target_cloud_file: 'cad.ply'" Launch state machine node. Inspection process starts clouds ROS topics: /target/cloud /source/cloud. target cloud CAD file, set param using_CAD true. roslaunch leica_point_cloud_processing leica_point_cloud_processing.launch using_CAD:=true FOD detection process ends, results clouds ROS topics. visualize detected FODs pointclouds RViz. Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.
This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

This package provides mapping from frequencies to

     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is

Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.

description: Node for using a video file as video topic source.
readme: movie_publisher package tools movie files ROS (playback, conversion bag files handles file formats system installation ffmpeg decode. Important: package meant work moviepy. due packaging issues, moviepy installed automatically dependency. fallback OpenCV, worse. install moviepy manually calling: sudo pip install moviepy rosdep install python-moviepy-pip Main tools movie_publisher_node: ROS node serves video file video topic source (sensor_msgs/Image friends). movie_publisher.launch: launch file convenient usage node. starting image_transport/republish node converts video stream raw compressed/theora. mode, convert video files bagfiles batch. movie_to_bag: batch script takes video input transforms bag file. add_movie_to_bag: batch script adds video topic existing bag file. Helper tools fix_bag_timestamps: batch script rewrites bag files message publication time message header. generate bagfile data high speed reprocess suitable publication timestamps. merge.py: copy srv_tools/merge.py indigo. movie_publisher_node node run backends moviepy opencv. moviepy strongly recommended, ffmpeg, versatile efficient. set backend param, autodetection run. Published topics movie (sensor_msgs/Image): published movie, raw format. Node-private parameters: movie_file (string, required): Path movie play. format ffmpeg decode. fps (float, optional): set, playback FPS (speeding up/slowing movie). start (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback start time. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set duration. (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback stop time affected start). expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start duration. duration (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback duration. set, duration counted clip, duration start clip. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start loop (bool, default False): loop movie node shut Exludes (bool, default False): True, movie processed published quickly waiting real time. timestamps resulting messages act “real-world-like” FPS means frames’ timestamps sec set fake_time_start timestamps non-zero time. Excludes loop. playback_rate (float, optional): set number, mode play fast rate (set rate number lose messages, image_transport/republish). fake_time_start (float, default timestamp message. frame_id (string, default frame_id messages’ headers. spin_after_end (bool, default False): True, rospy.spin() called movie published. verbose (bool, default False): True, logs info frame played. wait_after_publisher_created (float, default workaround case give subscribers time publisher created. Tweak number missing start messages. publisher_queue_size (int, default mode, queue_size movie publisher. backend (string, default “moviepy”): backend reading video. moviepy opencv. moviepy selected opencv support codecs). ffmpeg (string, default nonempty, specifies (absolute) path ffmpeg binary movie_publisher.launch launch file takes arguments node’s parameters. Additionally, takes arguments: transport (string, default 'raw'): Type image transport. launch file start image_transport/republish node publishes video encoded transport type. republished_topic_basename (string, default movie_$(arg transport)): Base topic serve republished messages. Full topic $(arg republished_topic_basename)/$(arg transport) $(arg republished_topic_basename) case raw transport). base topic movie_publisher_node subscribes (movie default). movie_to_bag Convert movie file bag file video topic. description: Mobile Robot Programming Toolkit (MRPT) version 1.5.x
readme: MRPT project Introduction Mobile Robot Programming Toolkit (MRPT) libraries aimed researchers mobile robotics computer vision. Libraries include SLAM solutions, geometry, SE(2)/SE(3) Lie groups, probability density functions (pdfs) points, landmarks, poses maps, Bayesian inference (Kalman filters, particle filters), image processing, obstacle avoidance, MRPT GUI apps Stereo camera calibration, dataset inspection, Resources Download latest unstable code git clone https://github.com/MRPT/mrpt.git --depth questions Google group stackoverflow tag mrpt!) Main project website, including sources Windows installer downloads API reference ROS packages: mrpt_navigation, mrpt_slam Bindings documentation (Python, Matlab) Source code dozens examples configuration files MRPT applications MRPT/share/mrpt/config_files sample datasets stored MRPT/share/mrpt/datasets. complete dataset repository online. contribute code feaures, bug fixes, Install Ubuntu PPA PPA branch sudo add-apt-repository ppa:joseluisblancoc/mrpt-1.5 sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install libmrpt-dev mrpt-apps Supported distributions: Ubuntu 14.04LTS Trusty. Ubuntu 16.04LTS Xenial. versions Ubuntu, recommended mrpt>=1.9.9. Build sources Minimum compiler requisites: mrpt (master branch): Ubuntu 16.04LTS Xenial: default gcc/clang versions Windows: Visual Studio mrpt maintenance mrpt-1.5 branch: Ubuntu 14.04LTS Trusty: default gcc/clang versions Windows: Visual Studio build Debian/Ubuntu follow steps full build docs online Windows instructions learn details. Install minimum recommended dependencies: sudo apt install build-essential pkg-config cmake libwxgtk3.0-dev libopencv-dev libeigen3-dev libgtest-dev Install additional dependencies enable MRPT features: sudo apt install libftdi-dev freeglut3-dev zlib1g-dev libusb-1.0-0-dev libudev-dev libfreenect-dev libdc1394-22-dev libavformat-dev libswscale-dev libassimp-dev libjpeg-dev libsuitesparse-dev libpcap-dev liboctomap-dev Build cmake usual: mkdir build build cmake License MRPT released BSD license.

description: omronsentech_camera package contains a basic functionality to access OMRON SENTECH Camera. This package requires installation of SentechSDK.
readme: ROS上のOMRON SENTECHカメラSDK omronsentech_cameraパッケージはオムロン 本パッケージ対応カメラはLinux版のSentechSDK対応カメラと同様です。 カメラの機能やGenICam GenApiノードのアクセスはSentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) GenICam GenApiノードとROSにあるノードとは全く別の物であることを注意下さい。 各GenICam GenApiノードは、汎用インタフェースを介してアクセス可能なカメラデバイスの機能またはレジスタに対応しています。GenICam GenApiの詳細は[GenICam GenApiドキュメント（英語）]を参照下さい。 本パッケージはUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-KineticとUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-Melodicで動作確認済みです。 SentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) モジュールを利用するため、本パッケージをインストールする際は事前にSentechSDKをインストールする必要があります。下記のURLでSentechSDK (USB3VisionとGigEVision)がダウンロードできます： https://sentech.co.jp/products/USB/software.html. 本パッケージは下記のROSパッケージが必要となります： camera_info_manager image_transport message_generation sensor_msgs std_msgs pluginlib nodelet roscpp roslaunch roslint rospy インストール手順として、catkinのワークスペースにomronsentech_camera.gitをクローンするかこのレポジトリに全ファイルをコピーして下さい。 通常のROSパッケージとしてパッケージをビルドする前にSentechSDKの環境変数の設定を確認して下さい: echo $STAPI_ROOT_PATH 環境変数が未設定の場合は、下記のコマンドを実行して下さい（SentechSDKを/opt/sentechにインストールした場合）： source /opt/sentech/.stprofile omronsentech_cameraの内部構成は以下のとおりです。 |-./README.md |-./README-ja.md |-./CMakeLists.txt |-./nodelet_plugins.xml |-./package.xml |-./rosdoc.yaml |-./doc/ |-./html/ |-./mainpage-en.md |-./mainpage-ja.md |-./manifest.yaml |-./examples/ |-./cpp/grabber.cpp |-./python/grabber.py |-./include/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.h |-./launch/ |-./*.launch |-./*.yaml |-./msg/ |-./*.msg |-./src/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.cpp |-./srv/ |-./*.srv docフォルダにあるドキュメントを生成する場合、omronsentech_cameraフォルダで下記のコマンドを実行下さい： rosdoc_lite doc 注意：Doxygenとrosdoc_liteパッケージが必要となります。 stcamera_nodeノード パッケージにあるstcamera_nodeはパラメータcamera_to_connectにより自動的に最初見つけたカメラ、指定したカメラや全カメラと接続し、画像を取得します。 stcamera_nodeノードの実行は次のコマンドのいずれかを実行下さい： rosrun omronsentech_camera stcamera_node roslaunch omronsentech_camera stcamera_node.launch ノードをstcamera_node.launchファイルスクリプトで起動するとカメラ接続パラメータがstcamera_node.yamlで簡単に設定できます。 camera_to_connect 接続可能なカメラの設定。このパラメーターは、stcamera_nodeが実行される前に設定する必要があります。 実行中にこのパラメーターを変更しても、その変更は反映されません。設定無しの場合、最初に見つけたカメラを接続します。”all”を設定する場合、見つけた全てのカメラを接続します。CAMERA_ID又はCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)を設定する場合、指定したカメラのみと接続します。例： camera_to_connect:[]: camera_to_connect:["all"]: camera_to_connect:["00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx","STC-MCS510U3V(00XXYY0)"]: MACアドレス「00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx」のGigEVisionカメラとシリアル番号「00XXYY0」のUSB3Visionカメラ(STC-MCS510U3V)のみ接続します。 camera_to_connect:["14210003XXYY"]: ID「14210003XXYY」のカメラのみ接続します. default_calibration_file 指定するキャリブレーションファイルがないカメラを利用するデフォールトキャリブレーションファイルパス。stcamera_nodeが実行中にこのパラメータが変更され、カメラもすでに接続されている場合は、変更を適用するためにカメラの画像取得を無効にして再度有効にして下さい。キャリブレーションファイルの作成と使い方は下記のROSのチュートリアルを参照下さい（英語）： http://wiki.ros.org/camera_calibration/Tutorials/MonocularCalibration File truncated lines full file

readme: seed_smartactuator_sdk ROS上のパッケージから本ライブラリをインクルードし、SEED-Driverへコマンドを送信するための一連サンプルを示す。 なお、PCからSEED-Driverへ直接指令を行う際は通信モジュール(CM4U/CMSU)が必要となる。 多軸ロボットへ指令する際は、aero3_command.hを用いる。詳細は seed_r7_ros_controllerを参考にされたい #include <ros/ros.h> #include "seed_smartactuator_sdk/seed3_command.h" int main(int argc, char **argv) ros::init(argc,argv,"cpp_sample_node"); ros::NodeHandle seed::controller::SeedCommand seed; seed.openPort("/dev/ttyACM0",115200); seed.openCom(); int std::array<int,3> motor_state; seed.runScript(id,1); seed.waitForScriptEnd(1); (ros::ok()) motor_state seed.getPosition(id); if(motor_state[0]) std::cout motor_state[2] std::endl; return Python #!/usr/bin/env python import rospy seed_smartactuator_sdk.seed3_command import SeedCommand main "__main__": rospy.init_node('python_sample_node') seed=SeedCommand() seed.open_port("/dev/ttyACM0",115200) seed.open_com() motor_state seed.run_script(id,1) seed.wait_for_script_end(1) rospy.is_shutdown(): motor_state seed.get_position(id) if(motor_state[0]): print motor_state[2]

description: The sr_gazebo_sim package

This is a ROS interface to the Shadow Robot's robotic hand. It

        contains an interface to Shadow Robot's muscle arm.
This package contains the actuator used in the hand model.

The sr_mechanism_controllers package contains different types of
        controllers for the etherCAT hand:(fake) calibration controllers,
        position controllers, velocity controllers, force controllers, ...

sr_mechanism_model contains the transmissions used in the robot model. We needed specific
        transmission as we're using our own actuator. We also needed to take care of the joint 0s
        which combine the distal and middle phalanges.

description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Install apt sudo apt install ros-kinetic-timed-roslaunch source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash source ~/<catkin_ws>/src git clone https://github.com/MoriKen254/timed_roslaunch.git ~/<catkin_ws> catkin_make source ~/<catkin_ws>/devel/setup.bash Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional argument </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

description:
A light-weight, Eigen-based
    C++ library for trajectory optimization for legged robots. 
    This library provides implementations for variables, costs and constraints
    that can be used to represent a legged locomotion problem. 
    The resulting Nonlinear Programming Problem (NLP) can then be solved with
    off-the-shelf solvers, e.g. Ipopt using the generic optimizer interface

description: This is a wrapper around the marker detection library ArUco.

The tuw_checkerboard package is designed to detect one 
    checkerboard and to estimate the pose of the checkerboard relative to the camera.
    The detection itself is based on the opencv functions for checkerboards.

The tuw_ellipses package contains a computer vision library which is able to detect ellipses within images.  
    The package is able to estimate the pose of the circle related to the ellipse the circle diameter as well as the camera parameter are known.
    A dynamic reconfigure interface allows the user to tune the parameter of the system to ones needs.
    But be aware that the pose of a projected circle within a image (ellipse) has two solutions and only one is published as TF.

description: UWSim is an UnderWater SIMulator for marine robotics research and development. UWSim visualizes an underwater virtual scenario that can be configured using standard modeling software. Controllable underwater vehicles, surface vessels and robotic manipulators, as well as simulated sensors, can be added to the scene and accessed externally through ROS interfaces. This allows to easily integrate the visualization tool with existing control architectures.

This package contains two small tools to help configure the navigation pipeline. The node min_max_finder.py prints the minimum and maximum linear and angular speed of the robot; the node acc_finder.py prints the time needed to achieve maximum speed.

description:
A multiplexer for command velocity inputs. Arbitrates incoming cmd_vel messages from several topics,
     allowing one topic at a time to command the robot, based on priorities. It also deallocates current
     allowed topic if no messages are received after a configured timeout. All topics, together with their
     priority and timeout are configured through a YAML file, that can be reload at runtime.

A ROS wrapper of the AprilTag 3 visual fiducial detection
    algorithm. Provides full access to the core AprilTag 3 algorithm's
    customizations and makes the tag detection image and detected tags' poses
    available over ROS topics (including tf). The core AprilTag 3 algorithm is
    extended to allow the detection of tag bundles and a bundle calibration
    script is provided (bundle detection is more accurate than single tag
    detection). Continuous (camera image stream) and single image detector nodes
    are available.

DAQ features: analog outputs optional MAX5500 chip), ADC inputs, GPIO PWM outputs, channels quadrature encoder decoding support, absolute encoder (EMS22A) input. Outputs feature optional watchdog timer resets output updated periodically (default period precompiled firmware atmega328P, recompiled larger versions pins. Valid pin numbers function pin configured fly host software. Encoder Channel pin Channels free GPIO pin. Compiling GNU/Linux Windows, CMake generate Makefiles project files usual. (Requires killing arduino_daq launch parameters). rosrun arduino_daq arduino_daq_node _SERIAL_PORT:=/dev/ttyUSB0 _ENC0_PIN_A:=2 _ENC0_PIN_B:=8 _ENC0_PIN_Z:=9 EMS22A absolute encoder: pin_cs); rosrun arduino_daq arduino_daq_node _SERIAL_PORT:=/dev/ttyUSB0 _ENCABS0_PIN_CS:=1 _ENCABS0_PIN_CLK:=2 _ENCABS0_PIN_DO:=2 _ENCABS_MEASURE_PERIOD_MS:=100 ROS graph Source code directories arduino_firmware: AtmelStudio project firmware. description: The choreo metapackage

this package is the central monitoring node that connects
    Choreo's specialized packages together. It receives requests from UI
    package and dispatch / receive computation request to responsible packages.

This package extends descartes planning package's ability to search joint path on a much larger ladder graph,
    by using sparse representation sparse ladder graph and run RRT* on this sparse graph.
    Then the sparse graph is extended back to ladder graph and run shortest path on it.
    This package is a part of the choreo assembly planning framework and contains
    several assembly application-dependent helper functions.

This package is responsible for the execution of process plans generated
    from core service clients.
    Currently it can stream generated trajectory from choreo to down stream trajectory simulation packages and
    at the moment vendor- and dof- agnostic.

    It has potential to be further connected to online control system, but might need more vendor-specific settings.

description:
This package contains a configurable node, services and a spawner script
        to start, stop and restart one or more controller plugins. Reusable
        controller types are defined for common Dynamixel motor joints. Both speed and
        torque can be set for each joint. This python package can be used by more
        specific robot controllers and all configurable parameters can be loaded
        via a yaml file.

description:

    MX-28, RX-64, EX-106 models. Hardware specific constants are
    defined for reading and writing information from/to Dynamixel
    servos. This low level package won't be used directly by most
    ROS users. The higher level dynamixel_controllers and specific
    robot joint controllers make use of this package.

hector_object_tracker is the core package of hector_worldmodel. It listens to percept message from
     detectors (e.g. heat signatures of persons or recognitions of other objects of interest in the
     scene) and fuses all information to a single worldmodel state. Objects will be tracked over time
     and their states can be influenced by a couple of services.

     The percept to object association problem is solved either automatically based on the Mahalanobis
     distance, or a unique object_id can be given in the percept message.

     If a hector_nav_msgs/GetDistanceToObstacle service is available, the object_tracker can optionally
     deduce the depth of objects in the scene by projection to the nearest obstacle (wall).

The hector_worldmodel stack helps to collect and fuse information about objects in the world.
    It is used by Team Hector Darmstadt to localize victims and QR codes in the Robocup Rescue
    scenario. See

hector_worldmodel_geotiff_plugins contains a plugin to visualize worldmodel objects (e.g. victims in a
     Search and Rescue scenario) in a geotiff map generated by

hector_worldmodel_msgs is a message package to comes with the hector_worldmodel stack.
     The messages can be used to send percepts from images (hector_worldmodel_msgs/ImagePercept) or other sources
     (hector_worldmodel_msgs/PosePercept) to the hector_object_tracker node. The tracker publishes model updates as
     hector_worldmodel_msgs/Object messages and latches the whole model state as a hector_worldmodel_msgs/ObjectModel message.

description: Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into a quaternion to represent the orientation of the device wrt the global frame. Based on the algorithm by Roberto G. Valenti etal. described in the paper "Keeping a Good Attitude: A Quaternion-Based Orientation Filter for IMUs and MARGs" available at http://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/15/8/19302 .

Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into an orientation. Based on code by Sebastian Madgwick, http://www.x-io.co.uk/node/8#open_source_ahrs_and_imu_algorithms.

description:
This is a ROS package for the metraTec Indoor Positioning System.
    You can use the regular IPS for zone location of your robot
    or the IPS+ products for 3D-position-estimation using UWB ranging.
readme: indoor_positioning ROS package metraTec Indoor Positioning System. regular IPS zone location robot IPS+ products 3D-position-estimation UWB ranging. image shows indoor positioning functionality. beacons environment localize receiver vicinity. IPS displays current (user-defined) zone receiver (green rectangle). Additionally, IPS+ position estimation, actual 3D-position receiver displayed (pink sphere). Installation ROS binaries (preferred method): Simply run command change distribution sudo apt-get install ros-ROSDISTRO-indoor-positioning source: binary release, older version ROS, manually build package clone repository source folder catkin workspace: git clone https://github.com/metratec/indoor_positioning.git YOUR_CATKIN_WS/src/indoor_positioning Install python dependencies pip install requirements.txt able package (catkin) package workspace. III. Usage ```receiver.py node handles communication receiver publishes incoming messages. run node order IPS IPS+ node. run ```positioning.py node IPS ```positioning_plus.py node IPS+ theoretically). Setup able nodes zone location (IPS) UWB positioning (IPS+) setup environment YAML configuration file. ```config/zones_template.yml information structure content config file. Launch files launch files test package default parameters. ```ips.launch file starts receiver IPS node zone location. ```ips_plus.launch file starts receiver IPS+ node UWB position estimation. TCP USB receiver connected. launching IPS node metraTec USB receiver roslaunch indoor_positioning ips.launch rec_name:=port rec_value:=/dev/ttyUSB0 launching IPS+ node TCP receiver roslaunch indoor_positioning ips_plus.launch rec_name:=host rec_value:=192.168.2.223 non-default parameters nodes set ROS parameter server write launch files set respective parameters launch files mentioned _Note:_ setting ```do_vis parameter launch files ```True simultaneously start rviz predefined settings display defined beacon positions, current zone and/or estimated receiver position. Nodes receiver.py node establish connection metraTec IPS receiver USB stick. pass USB port stick connected address regular receiver command line argument private parameter launch file. Running command line: rosrun indoor_positioning receiver.py TYPE REQUIRED OPTIONAL rosrun indoor_positioning receiver.py usb /dev/ttyUSB0 rosrun indoor_positioning receiver.py tcp Subscribed topics: ips/receiver/send (std_msgs/String): Message TCP serial connection IPS receiver USB stick Published topics: ips/receiver/raw (indoor_positioning/StringStamped): Raw messages received receiver USB stick Parameters: ~type (string, default=’tcp’): ‘tcp’ TCP receiver ‘usb’ metraTec USB stick ~host (string, default=’192.168.2.223): address connected receiver ~port type=’tcp’: int, default=10001; type=’usb’: string, default=’/dev/ttyUSB0): Port TCP connection ‘~type’ parameter set ‘tcp’, specifies USB port stick ~baudrate (int, default=115200): Baudrate serial communication USB receiver positioning.py (IPS) node perform indoor zone location metraTec IPS tracking system. description: ROS packages for the ROBOTIS MANIPULATOR-H (metapackage)

The manipulator_h_base_module package
    This package describes basic function to control ROBOTIS MANIPULATOR-H.
    This module is based on position control.
    We provides joint space and task space control (forward kinematics, inverse kinematics).

The manipulator_h_base_module_msgs package
    This package includes ROS messages and services for manipulator_h_base_module_msgs

The manipulator_h_bringup package
    This package includes launch file to describe robotis in Rviz.

The manipulator_h_description package
    This package includes URDF model of ROBOTIS MANIPULATOR-H.
    Additionally, we provide full kinematics and dynamics information of each link.

The manipulator_h_gazebo package
    This package provides GAZEBO simulation environment for ROBOTIS MANIPULATOR-H.
    We provides two controllers such as position and effort controllers.

The manipulator_h_gui package
    This package provides simple GUI to control ROBOTIS MANIPULATOR-H.
    This GUI is connected to manipulator_h_base_module.

The manipulator_h_kinematics_dynamics package
    This packages provides library of kinematics and dynamics information for ROBOTIS MANIPULATOR-H.
    Additionally, there are some function to calculate kinematics and dynamics.

The manipulator_h_manager package
    This package describes robot manager to execute manipulator_h_base_module.

This is a programming framework to facilitate application development involving robot swarms. It makes coding for swarms much easier by providing an adequate swarm-level abstraction, as well as tools for swarm management, various communication mechanisms and so on. It also provides essential data structures, such as Neighbor, Swarm, and Virtual Stigmergy, to the user. Most importantly, it is completely compatible with ROS Indigo and presented in the form of a C++ library, which means that all resources in the ROS ecosystem are still available to the user. It is currently  extensible to Opensplice DDS.
authors: Xuefeng Chang, Zhongxuan Cai, Yanzhen Wang, Xiaodong Yi

description: Implementation of the MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP)
readme: MultiWii Cleanflight Baseflight Communication Library library implements MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP) communicating MultiWii Cleanflight flight controller serial device. defines low-level API sending+encoding receiving+decoding MSP messages, high-level API subscriber pattern periodically request data call callback functions message received. communication tested MultiWii Arduino Nano achieve update rate approximately cycle time Betaflight Naze32 Rev6 update rates max. Installation Test Linux (Ubuntu Debian) install asio ninja: sudo apt install --no-install-recommends ninja-build libasio-dev check source code cmake compile: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release cmake --build build run program path serial device: ./msp_read_test /dev/ttyUSB0 Windows Requirements CMake asio: download asio-1.20.0.zip extract header files, C:\asio-1.20.0 Visual Build Tools: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/de/downloads/ Build Test open Developer Command Prompt Visual Studio change directory checked msp run: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DASIO_ROOT=C:\asio-1.20.0 cmake --build build test serial device: msp_read_test.exe Hardware Setup connect Arduino Naze32 boards built-in USB-to-Serial connector direct serial connection (RX->TX, TX->RX, GND->GND, VCC->VCC). option preferable high transfer rates, direct connection exposed transmission errors. MultiWii MSP update rate determined variable LOOP_TIME config.h #define LOOP_TIME sets loop time update rate Arduino boards integrated USB-to-serial converter auto-reset serial connection established. send messages opening connection request send response. prevent delay opening connection sending messages, disabling Data Terminal Ready (DTR) line, stty /dev/ttyUSB0 -hupcl. Cleanflight Betaflight change update rate serial task range set serial_update_rate_hz=2000, save increase serial baudrate Ports configuration tab Sending Receiving commands activate feature RX_MSP GUI: Configuration Receiver Receiver Mode MSP input CLI: execute feature RX_MSP Beginning Cleanflight Betaflight MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets insufficient flash memory naze32). verify MSP_RC messages return empty list channels. activate MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets, add #define USE_RX_MSP target.h, src/main/target/NAZE/target.h. FlightContoller API FlightContoller API user send/receive messages flight controller. Messages queried periodically, demand. Instantiation Instantiate setup FlightController class: #include <FlightController.hpp> fcu::FlightController fcu; connection setup fcu.connect("/dev/ttyUSB0", Periodic request messages Messages queried periodically handled automatically subscription. class method stand-alone function. Class method pattern Define class holds callback functions process information received message. function signature void <callback_name>(const <child_of_msp::Message>& class App public: void onStatus(const msp::Status& status) std::cout<<status; void onImu(const msp::Imu& imu) std::cout<<imu; Instantiate class callbacks register FCU desired update rate seconds: App app; File truncated lines full file

It is a function table that receives multipoint information,
  then controls the time of sending the target point and serves the Move_base
readme: multipoint_navigation_receiver 基于ROS的多点导航功能包 catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/SuperAruba/multipoint_navigation_receiver.git catkin_make 多点导航需要用到multipoint_navigation_receiver节点，由于考虑到目标点可能出现长时间存在障碍物的情况，故增加了一个判断地图中某点的障碍物信息的节点obstacle_server节点。 obstacle_server节点订阅了/move_base/global_costmap/costmap话题，使用服务机制，服务名称为/obstacle_information，请求服务时参数为地图某点的X坐标和Y坐标。该节点可以直接用于其他节点请求地图中的障碍物数据。该节点依赖于global_costmap，所以需要在global_costmap中加入obstacle layer。 multipoint_navigation_receiver节点使用时需要先开启obstacle_server节点，也使用服务机制，传入的参数为位置和姿态一一对应的两个数组（考虑到某些其他语言无法直接得到move_base_msgs::MoveBaseGoal数据类型）分别为geometry_msgs/Vector3[]和geometry_msgs/Quaternion[]。 BSD

description: myahrs_driver is a driver package for the WITHROBOT's myAHRS+. The myAHRS+ is a low cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) with USB/UART/I2C interface. The myAHRS+ board contains a 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, a 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer and a 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. The driver should also work with USB port.
readme: myahrs_driver Overview driver package WITHROBOT’s myAHRS+ http://www.lilliputdirect.com/odroid-myahrs lilliputdirect http://www.hardkernel.com/main/products/prdt_info.php?g_code=G141464363369 myAHRS+ cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) USB/UART/I2C interface. myAHRS+ board 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. driver work USB port. Axes Convention myAHRS+ board NED type. myahrs_driver contained package converts frame conventions ROS east north (ENU) convention hand rule) publishing msgs. driver coordinate frame http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html#axis-orientation information. left NED type IMU: x-north, y-east, z-down, relative magnetic north. ENU type IMU: x-east, y-north, z-up, relative magnetic north. Original Source original source support ROS) maintained github tutorials wiki page. visualization test 3D-box included original source. package myAHRS+ SDK https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus Video visualization demonstration RViz. Installation Install package: sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-myahrs-driver Install package github: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/robotpilot/myahrs_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Run Run driver rosrun myahrs_driver myahrs_driver _port:=/dev/ttyACM0 roslaunch myahrs_driver myahrs_driver.launch Nodes Official ROS documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/myahrs_driver Communication Protocol Manual Forum myAHRS+ protocol here(https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus/tree/master/tutorial). Forum myAHRS+ user here(http://forum.odroid.com/viewforum.php?f=109). description: The pioneer_mrs ROS package for Pioneer 3-AT Multi-Robot Systems
readme: pioneer_mrs Introduction ROS package Pioneer Multi-Robot Systems. flexible experimental platform multi-robot systems problem, feel free contact maintainer @hanzheteng open issue repository. ROS package Description (Features) Platform pioneer_mrs Support scientific computing library (numpy, scipy, algorithms Support kinds localization methods (odometry, VICON) Support kinds robot-level simulators (MobileSim, Gazebo) Visualization developing Pioneer Ubuntu LTS Robot Operating System (kinetic) ROSARIA package (git-commit-aa8d5f7) ARIA lib (2.9.1a-ubuntu16-i386) Usage Running Robots System Configuration package drive robots, configure platform steps: machines robots laptop) installed Ubuntu ROS Kinetic robots (onboard computer, installed catkin_make-ed ARIA lib ROSARIA package machines static address able automatically connect local WiFi Write hostname information /etc/hosts file machines commander (laptop) able SSH robots public key password) Onboard computers able access microcontroller serial post (add user dialout group) detailed information set system, refer wiki page. suggestions system development. install teamviewer software laptop robots remote login onboard computers. IDE, recommend RoboWare Studio, designed ROS developers. alternative KDevelop, cross-platform IDE. Package Dependencies git clone repository, install ARIA lib git clone ROSARIA package catkin_make ROSARIA package ARIA library. clone repository git clone https://github.com/hanzheteng/pioneer_mrs.git catkin_make package workspace. Notice: catkin_make ROSARIA package clone package, report errors. catkin_make pioneer_mrs_generate_messages catkin_make package, compiler able find header file messages. Optional package: VICON motion capture system, git clone catkin_make vicon_bridge package. Sample Usage Basically steps: launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Vicon: launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch pose:=vicon launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Running MobileSim Simulator ROSARIA package connect MobileSim simulator. install MobileSim laptop. Basically steps: run MobileSim simulator spawn robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs mobilesim.launch launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch machine:=mobilesim launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Running Gazebo Simulator ROSARIA package Gazebo charge publishing odom information dealing cmd_vel commands. Gazebo installed default ROS choosing desktop-full install. simulations Pioneer robots, models URDF files supplied Mobile Robots git clone amr-ros-config meta-package. Basically steps: run Gazebo simulator spawn robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs gazebo.launch launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch pose:=gazebo machine:=gazebo launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Notice: real robots, movement commands execute WatchDog timeout mechanism; gazebo simulator, robots moving direction. press space bar (stop command) stop Single Robot Recoverary Optional launch file robot1): launch robot roslaunch pioneer_mrs single-robot.launch hostname:=robot1 launch robot Vicon roslaunch pioneer_mrs single-robot.launch hostname:=robot1 pose:=vicon launch robot MobileSim simulator File truncated lines full file

Sockets teleoperation: Controls robot remotely throught socket commands move robot web interface internet/LAN). roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch expected commands “left” default, script listens port robot moves seconds. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _port:=12345 _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 Command line teleoperation move robot command line throught terminal ssh roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_commandline.launch _direction:=forward expected commands (_direction argument) “left” default, robot moves seconds speed. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _direction:=backward _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 robot Pionner-compatible? package compatible robot ROS long velocity commands published /cmd_vel topic section). modify .launch scripts remove pionner_bringup call, execute python scripts located /nodes folder. issues Velocity command topic default, scripts publish velocity commands /cmd_vel topic. case velocity commands topic Pionner-compatible robots, remap velocity topic /cmd_vel change topic python scripts /nodes folder issues report problem issues panel File truncated lines full file

description: Package to access the Raspberry Pi Camera from ROS.
readme: raspicam_node ROS node Raspberry Camera Module. Works versions module. recommend cameras auto gain, general image quality Installation binary https://packages.ubiquityrobotics.com/ follow instructions add repository. run sudo apt install ros-kinetic-raspicam-node Build Intructions build source binary follow section. node primarily supported ROS Kinetic, Ubuntu instuctions presume. catkin_ws ~/catkin_ws/src. Download source node running git clone https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/raspicam_node.git dependencies recognized ros, create file /etc/ros/rosdep/sources.list.d/30-ubiquity.list add yaml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/UbiquityRobotics/rosdep/master/raspberry-pi.yaml run rosdep update. Install ros dependencies, ~/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro=kinetic Compile code catkin_make. Running Node node built, run launch file. camera, run roslaunch raspicam_node camerav2_1280x960.launch camera, run roslaunch raspicam_node camerav1_1280x720.launch rqt_image_view connected computer view published image. Configuring node dynamic reconfigure raspicam_node supports dynamically reconfiguring camera parameters. Run dynamic reconfigure node connected computer: rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure bring user interface Paramaters dynamically adjusted interface. Troubleshooting user video group running groups|grep video. error Failed create camera component, camera cable properly seated ends, cable missing pins. publish rate image network expected, resolution reduce amount bandwidth required. Node Information Topics: ~/image/compressed: Publishes sensor_msgs/CompressedImage jpeg camera module. ~/image: Publishes sensor_msgs/Image camera module parameter enable_raw set). ~/motion_vectors: Publishes raspicam_node/MotionVectors camera module parameter enable_imv set). ~/camera_info: Publishes sensor_msgs/CameraInfo camera info frame. Services: ~/set_camera_info: update calibration info camera. Parameters: ~private_topics (bool): default topics private, meaning node added front topic don’t topics private, set parameter “true”. parameter present order compatibility. ~camera_frame_id frame): frame identifier associate camera. ~camera_info_url: URL camera calibration .yaml file. ~camera_name (string): camera, match camera_info file. File truncated lines full file

description: Messages, serivices and actions for SawYer roch
Metapackage for SawYer roch robot software

A controller ensuring the safe operation of roch.

    The SafetyController keeps track of ult, psd and cliff events. In case of the first three,
    roch is commanded to move back. 
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (cliff, psd and ult etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!
    Refrence with yujinrobot' kobuki.

Ult/psd/cliff to pointcloud:
    Publish Ult, cliff, and psd sensors events as points in a pointcloud, so navistack can use them
    for poor-man navigation. Implemented intended to run together with roch_base.

A hub acts as a shared key-value store for multiple ros 
    systems (primarily used by gateways).
maintainers: Daniel Stonier, Jihoon Lee, Piyush Khandelwal
authors: Daniel Stonier, Jihoon Lee, Piyush Khandelwal

description:
Client api for discovery and connection to rocon hubs. It also has
    a few convenience api for manipulating data on the hub.

This doesn't do everything you need for multimaster, however it does
    provide the building blocks common to most or all multimaster systems.
    In particular, it provides the gateway model, which is an upgrade on
    old foreign_relay and master_sync concepts.
maintainers: Daniel Stonier, Jihoon Lee, Piyush Khandelwal
authors: Daniel Stonier, Jihoon Lee, Piyush Khandelwal

description: Rocon test framework (i.e. multi-launch rostest framework).

description: A web interface for exploring the ROS graph

Tested Indigo, works newer ROS distros. Running ROS Explorer simple webpage, serve www directory python SimpleHTTPServer running websocket server roslaunch rosbridge_server rosbridge_websocket.launch. provide launch files convenience. roslaunch ros_explorer ros_explorer.launch: Runs Python server www directory opens ROS Explorer web browser. xdg-open python SimpleHTTPServer. website served http://localhost:8595. roslaunch ros_explorer ros_explorer_websocket.launch: runs websocket server. User notes default, websocket URL determined host webpage served localhost, robot.university.edu). scroll horizontally view names cut holding Shift scrolling. Press Ctrl+F search Developing ROS Explorer built Polymer. Install Node.js nvm don’t Install nvm nvm install node Install Polymer CLI don’t npm install polymer-cli Download frontend dependencies: frontend bower update Run local development server: polymer serve Visit http://localhost:8081 web browser. Robot web server ROS Explorer works RWS.

description: The ros_opcua_communication mate-package are ROS bidings for different open-source OPC-UA implementations. Currently following libraries are supported: FreeOpcUa and python-opcua.
The ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua package implements bindings for freeopcua - Open Source C++ OPC-UA Server and Client Library.
readme: ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua Ths package OPC client implementation ROS. client connect OPC server access simple data started ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua start OPC client implementation repository OPC server started. testing server freopcua libraray. start commands: roscd ./build/ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua/freeopcua/bin/example_server start client roslaunch ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua client.launch NOTE: server run computer server. running diffrent computers, localhost insert DNS address computer server running. recommended cross check values server UaExpert OPC client. examples provide nodeId service calls qualifiedName required left Reading Writing Variables services connect server, read write data: rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/connect "endpoint: 'opc.tcp://localhost:4840'" rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/read "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2001' qualifiedName: 'MyVariable'" rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/write "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2001' qualifiedName: 'MyVariable' data: {type: 'uint32', bool_d: false, int8_d: uint8_d: int16_d: uint16_d: int32_d: uint32_d: int64_d: uint64_d: float_d: double_d: string_d: lines OPC server variable Objects->NewObject->MyVariable (ATTENTION: fast check server seconds!) Subscribe Variable rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/connect "endpoint: 'opc.tcp://localhost:4840'" rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/subscribe "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2001' qualifiedName: 'MyVariable' callback_topic: 'topic'" Start echo created callback topic topic: rostopic echo /opcuopcua_client/topic output seconds. Exmple: Method Call rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/call_method "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=99' qualifiedName: 'NewObject' method: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2003' qualifiedName: 'MyMethod' data: {type: bool_d: false, int8_d: uint8_d: int16_d: uint16_d: int32_d: uint32_d: int64_d: uint64_d: float_d: double_d: string_d: result “MyMethod called!” output terminal server started. Compiling freeopcua libraray needed automatically) package implements bindings freeopcua Open Source OPC-UA Server Client Library. build FreeOpcUa commands (asume FreeOpcUa folder): mkdir build build cmake change FreeOpcUa_LIBRARIES variable compatible environment. Enjoy!

Usage general, process single component. component operations bare interface RTT service. “lookupTransform” Lookup transform source target. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “lookupTransformAtTime” Lookup transform source target specific time. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “common_time”, ros::Time common time transform computed “broadcastTransform” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transform”, geometry\_msgs::TransformStamped “broadcastTransforms” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transforms”, std::vector<geometry_msgs::TransformStamped> operations, instantiate rtt_tf::RTT_TF component, connect components intend import("rtt_ros"); Import load component ros.import("rtt_tf"); loadComponent("tf","rtt_tf::RTT_TF"); Connect operations component's service component's required service (easily created API shown section) loadComponent("my_component","MyComponent"); connectServices("my_component","tf"); API rtt_tf/tf_interface.h header includes rtt_tf::TFInterface class adds required service RTT component. easily connecting component. create RTT service requester #include <rtt_tf/tf_interface.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: rtt_tf::TFInterface public: MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name), Add services tf_(this) bool startHook() ready connected return tf_.ready(); void updateHook() Lookup transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped tform tf_.lookupTransform("/world","/my_frame");

readme: Rviz Visual Tools API wrapper displaying shapes meshes Rviz helper functions publish markers. displaying debugging data. advanced robot visualization features, moveit_visual_tools builds class. package includes: Rviz Panel GUI step code debugging testing Rviz-based keyboard control stepping application Easy helper functions visualizing Rviz fast Basic geometric markers Rviz complex geometric shapes coordinate frames, framed boxes, planes, paths, graphs Ability quickly choose standard colors sizes Tools ensure proper connection Rviz publishing visualizations Shortcuts convert types points poses ROS msgs, Eigen, Batch publishing capabilities reduce throttling ROS messages publishing helper class interactive marker helper class Developed Dave Coleman Correll Robotics Lab, University Colorado Boulder Andy McEvoy Travis ROS Buildfarm AMD64 Xenial Debian Build ROS Buildfarm AMD64 Xenial Devel Build Install Ubuntu Debian sudo apt-get install ros-kinetic-rviz-visual-tools Build Source Clone repository catkin workspace, rosdep install tool automatically download dependencies. Depending current version ROS, rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro kinetic Quick Start Demo random shapes generated Rviz, launch Rviz: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo_rviz.launch start demo: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo.launch Code API Doxygen documentation Usage assume helper functions class. functions assume publishing transforms world frame call /odom). Initialize Add includes: #include <rviz_visual_tools/rviz_visual_tools.h> Add class’s member variables: visualizing rviz rviz_visual_tools::RvizVisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; class’ constructor add: visual_tools_.reset(new rviz_visual_tools::RvizVisualTools("base_frame","/rviz_visual_markers")); Change parameter robot’s base frame, parameter you’d Rviz marker ROS topic. Tools code easily debug code visual markers Rviz Start rviz create marker ‘Add’ button bottom Choose marker topic topic constructor. Code snippet create pose xyz rotate pose degrees axis. publish pose arrow visualziation Rviz. Rviz fixed frame chosen code. Create pose Eigen::Affine3d pose; pose Eigen::AngleAxisd(M_PI/4, Eigen::Vector3d::UnitY()); rotate axis degrees pose.translation() Eigen::Vector3d( translate x,y,z Publish arrow vector pose ROS_INFO_STREAM_NAMED("test","Publishing Arrow"); visual_tools_->publishArrow(pose, rviz_visual_tools::RED, rviz_visual_tools::LARGE); File truncated lines full file

description:
The package provides implementation of several 2D laser-based simultaneous
    localization and mapping (SLAM) algorithms (tinySLAM, vinySLAM, GMapping)
    created with the SLAM constructor framework. The framework provides common
    SLAM components that may help to develop custom SLAM algorithms and can be
    accessed by provided links.
readme: SLAM Constructor Framework SLAM constructor framework common functionality classes create custom SLAM algorithms laser scan-based methods supported). includes implementation SLAM algorithms: tinySLAM, vinySLAM GMapping. show framework usage base SLAM algorithm. Hardware Requirements Current implementation requires odometry data laser scans ROS topics Subscribed topics). supposes laser scanner fixed robot mounted horizontally. Examples algorithms usage algorithm supplied launch-files MIT Stata Center Willow Garage datasets give idea launch algorithms. _mit_ launch-files general case data dataset require preprocessing. tinySLAM launched roslaunch slam_constructor tiny_mit_run.launch path:=[path dataset] order run algorithm data received real time remove dataset player node launch file, sensor data subscribed topics. description: SMACHA (short for "State Machine Assembler", pronounced "smasha") aims at distilling the task-level simplicity of SMACH into compact YAML-based scripts in the foreground, while retaining all of its power and flexibility in Jinja2-based templates and a custom code generation engine in the background.
readme: SMACHA SMACHA YAML Jinja2-based meta-scripting, templating, code generation engine SMACH. SMACH exceptionally comprehensive task-level architecture state machine construction ROS-based robot control systems. terms power flexibility, task-level simplicity obfuscated script-level boilerplate code, intricate structure lack code reuse state machine prototypes. SMACHA (short “State Machine Assembler”, pronounced “smasha”) aims distilling task-level simplicity SMACH compact YAML-based scripts foreground, retaining power flexibility Jinja2-based templates custom code generation engine background. Overview SMACHA Scripts SMACHA scripts YAML files describe SMACHA generate SMACH code. “Nesting State Machines” SMACH Tutorials SMACHA script: Nesting State Machines Tutorial SMACHA script. sm_top template: Base manifest: smach_tutorials node_name: smach_example_state_machine outcomes: [outcome5] states: BAS: template: Bas transitions: {outcome3: template: StateMachine outcomes: [outcome4] transitions: {outcome4: outcome5} states: FOO: template: Foo transitions: {outcome1: BAR, outcome2: outcome4} BAR: template: Bar transitions: {outcome1: FOO} demonstrates reduction lines raw SMACH code lines SMACHA script. Nice. Base Variables base script specifies variables: state machine, template: base template, manifest (optional): optional ROS manifest, node_name: ROS node, outcomes: list outcomes, states: list constituent states. states base script turn, similar variables discussed sub-sections. States state, including base, template respective code generated. States lists transition order nested SMACH tutorials combinations template state specifications Outcomes state outcomes list base state machine container state. Transitions state transitions associative array (hash/dictionary) state. Remappings Input output remappings user data shown SMACH User Data Tutorial details) associative array state. Jinja2 Templating Jinja2 powerful template engine Python. Jinja2 templates code generated SMACHA scripts. Base template Base.tpl file ```python “Utils.tpl” import render_userdata set defined_headers block base_header #!/usr/bin/env python base_header endblock base_header File truncated lines full file

description: SMACHA ROS is a package that provides ROS integration for SMACHA. Its main component is a SMACHA server that allows for templates to be loaded from disparate packages and accessed from anywhere within the ROS network and provides services for each of the main SMACHA functions.
